canonical_title: OpenAI and Google AI services reached subsidiaries of US-blacklisted Chinese groups
aliases: Alibaba Baidu Tencent Singapore subsidiaries AI access
treatment: Treat as a distinct export-control and access-governance story. Do not repeat unless a company, US agency, or affected Chinese group confirms new restrictions, enforcement, suspensions, legal changes, or materially clarifies which services and subsidiaries were involved.
known_facts: Financial Times reported that OpenAI and Google supplied advanced AI services to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent while the parent groups were on a US Defense Department blacklist.
The reported transactions were legal under the existing framework but exposed a gap between hardware export controls and controls on access to advanced AI software services.
The report said OpenAI suspended access for some Alibaba-linked users amid model-distillation concerns.
Anthropic has taken a stricter position by barring Chinese-controlled entities from using its models.
reinclude_only_if: OpenAI or Google publishes a formal policy change or identifies affected accounts and services.
The US government changes export-control or blacklist enforcement rules for AI software or model access.
Alibaba, Baidu, Tencent, or their subsidiaries issue a material response.
Credible reporting establishes scale, dates, model names, revenue, or government involvement not previously known.
notes: Selected as a new night delta because it adds previously unknown factual reporting about legal access through overseas subsidiaries and an AI-software export-control gap.
